rolling_30d.summary: The Radon Guys' local pages site delivered 332 sessions over the last 30 days, representing a sharp -27.5% MoM decline but a remarkable +427.0% YoY surge. Despite the month-over-month contraction, engagement rate improved by 3.2 percentage points to 20.5%, signaling healthier quality traffic—only 68 of 332 sessions engaged, but those that did showed intent. Direct traffic dominates at 90.1% of sessions (299) with explosive +1200% YoY growth, though it also fell -27.1% MoM, suggesting seasonal or campaign-driven volatility. High-value conversions show mixed signals: mainsite_clicks held steady at 39 events (-2.5% MoM, +34.5% YoY), but critical phone calls plummeted to just 5 events (-61.5% MoM, -54.5% YoY)—a concerning trend for a service-based business. The bright spot is store_locate interactions, which grew +9.1% MoM and surged +200.0% YoY to 24 events, indicating strong location-specific intent. Year-to-date momentum remains exceptional at +907.8% vs. 2025 (1,552 vs. 154 sessions), though March's 344 sessions signal a deceleration pattern after January's 784-session peak.

quick_wins.title: Bowling Green, KY page driving outsized engagement
quick_wins.detail: /kentucky/bowling-green generated 9 sessions with 88.9% engagement rate—the highest of all location pages—suggesting this market is particularly receptive to the service offering.

quick_wins.title: Store locator momentum accelerating
quick_wins.detail: store_locate events grew +9.1% MoM and +200.0% YoY to 24 events, proving the location-finder tool is resonating and driving high-intent traffic toward conversion funnels.

quick_wins.title: Mainsite clicks stable despite traffic decline
quick_wins.detail: mainsite_clicks held at 39 events with only -2.5% MoM decline while overall sessions fell -27.5%, indicating visitors who land on local pages are increasingly likely to navigate to the main site—a sign of effective funnel design.

potential_warnings.title: Phone calls in freefall
potential_warnings.detail: calls dropped to just 5 events, down -61.5% MoM and -54.5% YoY—a critical conversion metric for a service business. This suggests either landing page messaging is not driving phone intent, or call-to-action placement/visibility needs immediate review.

potential_warnings.title: Month-over-month traffic contraction accelerating
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell -27.5% MoM after Q1's peak in January (784 sessions); March dropped to 344 sessions. If this trend continues into April, the YTD growth narrative will weaken significantly.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search declining YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search delivered only 30 sessions (9% of total) with -30.2% MoM and -18.9% YoY decline. Over-reliance on direct traffic (90.1%) and weak organic performance suggests SEO or content visibility issues.

potential_warnings.title: Homepage engagement concentrated but limited in reach
potential_warnings.detail: The homepage (/) captured 177 sessions and 36 engaged sessions, yet engagement rate of 20.3% lags behind location-specific pages like Bowling Green (88.9%). Homepage messaging may need localization or clearer CTAs.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revive phone call conversion pathway
biggest_opportunities.detail: With calls at an all-time low (5 events, -61.5% MoM), audit landing page CTA prominence, phone number visibility, and click-to-call functionality across all location pages. Test click-to-call buttons above the fold and implement local number formatting.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale Bowling Green success across other location pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Bowling Green's 88.9% engagement rate vastly outperforms other locations. Analyze messaging, CTA structure, and layout on that page and replicate it across underperforming location pages like Tennessee/Columbia (0% engagement) and Tennessee/Dickson (25%).

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild organic search traffic and reduce direct traffic dependency
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search is down -30.2% MoM and -18.9% YoY to just 30 sessions; 90.1% reliance on direct traffic creates channel risk. Invest in location-based SEO content and link-building for high-intent local keywords in service areas.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand store_locate promotion and integration
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate events grew +200% YoY and +9.1% MoM (24 events), proving strong location-finder intent. Promote this tool more prominently on the homepage and in navigation, and ensure it links directly to booking or call-initiation flows.

last_calendar_month.summary: The Radon Guys experienced a significant traffic decline MoM (-18.9%) in March 2026, dropping from 424 to 344 sessions, though YoY growth remains exceptional at +437.5% versus March 2025's 64 sessions. Despite the monthly pullback, engagement rate improved +4.0 pp to 20.9%, signaling that remaining traffic is more qualified. Direct traffic dominates at 89.5% of sessions with extraordinary YoY growth (+1300%), indicating strong brand awareness and repeat visitation, though it declined slightly MoM (-19.2%). High-value conversion signals show mixed momentum: mainsite_clicks grew +16.7% MoM and +44.8% YoY (42 events), while critical phone calls collapsed -61.5% MoM and -54.5% YoY (5 events). Store location finder interactions surged +36.8% MoM and +271.4% YoY (26 events), representing the strongest conversion intent signal. YTD 2026 performance is outstanding at 1,552 sessions (+907.8% vs YTD 2025's 154), though the three-month trajectory shows deceleration: January's +1963.2% YoY growth moderated to March's +437.5%, suggesting market saturation or seasonal normalization.

quick_wins.title: Store locator momentum accelerating
quick_wins.detail: store_locate events grew +36.8% MoM and +271.4% YoY, demonstrating strong local intent signals; this is the highest-performing high-intent conversion event on the property.

quick_wins.title: Homepage engagement remaining stable
quick_wins.detail: The / landing page generated 186 sessions with a 20.4% engagement rate and sustained +1.1% MoM growth despite overall traffic decline, with YoY growth of +708.7% showing foundational brand strength.

quick_wins.title: Mainsite clicks trending upward
quick_wins.detail: mainsite_clicks grew +16.7% MoM to 42 events and +44.8% YoY, indicating deeper funnel progression and users advancing toward primary conversion destinations.

potential_warnings.title: Phone call conversions in freefall
potential_warnings.detail: calls dropped -61.5% MoM (from 13 to 5 events) and -54.5% YoY, representing the steepest decline among high-value conversion events and a critical funnel leakage point.

potential_warnings.title: Monthly traffic deceleration trend
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell -18.9% MoM and YoY growth compressed from January's +1963.2% to March's +437.5%, signaling potential market saturation or seasonal decline after Q1 spike.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search underperforming
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search contributed only 33 sessions (9.6% of traffic) and declined -13.2% MoM and -15.4% YoY, suggesting SEO visibility or keyword strategy may need optimization.

potential_warnings.title: Multiple location pages showing zero engagement
potential_warnings.detail: Pages like /tennessee/murfreesboro, /tennessee/bell-buckle, and /tennessee/columbia recorded 3–4 sessions each with 0% engagement, indicating potential irrelevant traffic or poor landing page relevance.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect phone call conversion funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: With calls down -61.5% MoM, audit call-to-action placement, button visibility, and mobile usability across top landing pages; prioritize the homepage (/) and /kentucky/bowling-green where engagement is strong.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand organic search investment
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search represents only 9.6% of sessions despite a +437.5% YoY baseline; developing location-specific content and radon-service keywords could capture high-intent search volume and reduce reliance on Direct traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Leverage store locator success into booking actions
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate events are thriving (+271.4% YoY, 26 events), but no booking/scheduling events are tracked; implement post-locator prompts to convert location-finders into appointment bookings and capture the momentum.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize underperforming local landing pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /tennessee/murfreesboro, /tennessee/bell-buckle, and /tennessee/columbia show 0% engagement; consolidate, refresh content, or redirect to higher-performing location pages like /kentucky/bowling-green (100% engagement).
